description_long: This object is a historical naval cannon mounted on a wooden wheeled carriage. The cannon barrel is made of blackened metal, featuring a slightly flared muzzle and reinforced rings along its length. The carriage is constructed from thick, dark wood planks, held together with metal bands and bolts, and is equipped with four solid metal-rimmed wheels for mobility. A length of rope is attached to the carriage, likely for maneuvering or securing the cannon. The overall appearance is robust and utilitarian, with visible wear and patina on both the metal and wood surfaces. This type of cannon would have been used in historical combat scenarios, particularly on ships or in fortified urban settings.
description: A black metal cannon mounted on a sturdy wooden wheeled carriage, with metal fittings and a rope attached. The design is robust and historically styled, showing signs of wear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the black cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and all four metal-rimmed wheels.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular muzzle of the cannon and the symmetrical arrangement of the carriage and wheels.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the cannon barrel, carriage, and rope attachment, with similar features to view 0 but from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the breech of the cannon and the back structure of the wooden carriage, with the wheels and rope visible.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
attribute_armor_plating: None visible; the carriage is made of wood and the cannon is solid metal with no additional armor plating.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; the cannon and carriage are finished in solid black and brown tones.
